representatives.text: ​@flopasen It is absolutely wild to me how many people are still pumping this line that you can't build production code with AI. It is true in the sense that a person with no understanding of architecture, infrastructure, or security can't vibe code a good serious app over a long

@seeknndestroy420 AI is not a substitute for skill and experience, at least not here and now. But in the hands of an experienced developer, that is eager to seriously engage in acquiring the skills to use it properly, it's a tremendous game changer in terms of productivity.
It to

@aitoolcorner You read the code but the code always gives you suggestions and causes that it is hard to go out of the box. If you need to implement something from scratch, all ways are possible. AI is good if you need boilerplate but not to implement sophisticated solutions. This

​@Mr_Big_Head  You keep reducing your position to ‘AI is useful,’ but that was never the contested part. The part I pushed back on was the implication that adjacent technical expertise is basically irrelevant here. It isn’t. A real engineer using AI is not the same as a random pe

@sousoux I've made my share, and I've worked alongside many human developers who have made mistakes. AI isn't perfect, but neither are humans. In time (and sooner rather than later) it will be less error-prone than humans. We know for sure that it's already many orders of magnitu

I'm with you. It's only really good on the surface. But the devil is in the details. The quality of the agentic AI drops significantly in brownfield codebases. It shines in throw away ptoof of concepts. That's about it. So the best thing is to keep the tasks slow and steer well.

They just sped it up, in the tiktok style, but don't realize how crappy it sounds. Many (too many) podcasters do it, but this is so dumb... People who want to save 20 seconds on the video, can speed it up themselves, why do they have to ruin the audio quality for everybody is bey
i always speed up virtually every single youtube vid i view, but not this one. i think it's already sped up and i'm totally ok with that.

@michaelm8499 It is, and it is incredibly annoying. I mean we can speed the video up ourselves if we want to, thank you very much, but if it comes pre-sped up, there's no way to remove the audio artifacts of that.

@michaelm8499 that would be freshening… I swear YouTubers were intentionally talking slower and slower… but maybe that was just YouTube intentionally slowing down their videos, so the YouTubers are now intentionally speed up their vids to compensate for the YouTube slowdown.
Yeah, I had to drop from x2 to x1.5 lol though to be fair, I swear most YouTubers actually reduce their normal speaking speed… or maybe YouTube does it on their behalf.
Haha I listen to everything at 2x. Slowed this one down to 1.5 and might need to creep back toward 1.25. Thanks to Andrew for talking at speed!
